payload.park_reason: register-rotation wall — carries 1 cheat-asm barrier (asm volatile("" : "=r"(t0) : "0"(t0));). Source removal causes GCC to coalesce t0+t1 into same register (s0) since t0 dies at func_80036F28(t0) before t1 is born. Target wants t0 in s0 (sequential with ret_a) and t1 in s1 (sequential with t0_2). Tested in session 2026-06-06: (a) sibling-style single-variable reuse (s0=t0/ret_a, s1=t1/t0_2) produces structurally identical asm but with 3-way register rotation (arg0→s2 not s3, frame_off→s1 not s2, t1→s3 not s1); (b) adding explicit frame_off local for arg0+ret_a didn't shift allocation. The barrier forces t0 alive past t1's birth, blocking coalesce. No pure-C source structure tested extends t0's natural lifetime past func_80036F28(t0) without semantic invention. No semantic use of t0 exists after that point; combine-folded uses (t0-t0, t0&0, 0x5E+(t0-t0)) all collapse before flow.c. Resume avenues: (1) instrumented cc1 ALLOCDBG to confirm exact priority ordering — may reveal pseudo number / decl-order lever; (2) directed permuter from clean single-function target with PERM_GENERAL on variable decl order / type. NOT canonical-asm-candidate (thin wrapper, no GTE/BIOS/HW).

The do-while-0 currently keeps the floor at 3 via opaque scheduling/RA effect (no branch-invert pattern, so NOT a sanctioned [[do-while-zero-exception]] use case).
